summary: Phase C SHIPPER frontend wiring tested via Playwright. CRITICAL JSX syntax error in /app/frontend/app/shipper-create.tsx (lines 1340-1380 fallback breakdown branch had adjacent JSX without parent) was blocking the entire web bundle (Metro bundling FAILED, /shipper-login showed blank screen with 'Cannot read properties of undefined' and 500 on resource). Fixed the malformed else-branch so it wraps content in a single <View style={styles.breakdown}> and adds the missing Base-fee row. After fix: app loads, login works, the multi-step create-shipment flow reaches the Price step, the new POST /api/shipper/quote/recommend is called (response captured: 200 OK with breakdown_lines, marketplace.heat, savings, environment) and the UI renders every Phase C element: total, Market Heat chip ('❄️ Uusimaa · Cold'), green savings box ('You save €9 (23%) vs typical freight (€39)'), environmental line ('🌱 ~0 kg CO₂ · 0 empty km · 0 L fuel saved'), and the transparent server breakdown (Base booking fee €25 · box_truck / Distance 0.0 km × €1.60/km €0 / Weight · Medium +10.0% €2.50 / Supply & demand −15.0% −€4.12 / Fuel adjustment +8.0% €1.87 / Minimum price floor €30.0 min €4.75 / Total €30.00). NO huge linear 'weight surcharge' row — the new weight-category model is wired end-to-end. Regression: bonus field still adds to total correctly (€30 → €35 with €5 bonus). Booking submission (POST /api/shipper/shipments) was not end-to-end captured in this run due to a flaky Sign-In click in the final attempt (token wasn't persisted in that session), but earlier attempts in the same iteration confirmed auth + recommend work; the submit code path is unchanged from iter47/48 which already passed.

issues: When pickup/dropoff are typed manually without selecting on map, coords fall back to Helsinki center so distance=0 → environment shows '0 kg CO₂ · 0 empty km · 0 L fuel saved'. Cosmetic only — real users picking on the map will see non-zero values. Consider hiding the env line when distance_km<1 km.

action_items: Main agent: Phase C wiring is functionally correct on the happy path. The fallback breakdown branch (when server doesn't return breakdown_lines) was malformed JSX — I patched it to compile, but please review the patched block (shipper-create.tsx lines ~1340–1381) for label/translation polish. Server always returns breakdown_lines today so this branch only fires as a safety net.
Optional: hide/condense the environment savings line when computed distance_km is ~0 (e.g. coords fell back to default) so users don't see '0 kg CO₂ · 0 empty km · 0 L fuel saved'.
critical_code_review_comments: shipper-create.tsx is 2324 lines — well over the 700-line guideline. Strongly recommend splitting into per-step subcomponents (Step1Pickup, Step2Dropoff, Step3Package, Step4Price, Step5When, Step6Review) and a usePricing hook. Future edits like the Phase C breakdown branch are exactly where a 2.3k-line file produces JSX bugs that aren't caught locally.
fetchQuote useCallback has 'BASE' in its dep array (an outer module constant) — harmless but flagged by eslint react-hooks/exhaustive-deps.
Two TextInput onChangeText handlers use the parameter name 't' which shadows the i18n function 't' from useTranslation in the same scope (e.g. line ~1389). Not a current bug but a footgun.
updated_files: /app/frontend/app/shipper-create.tsx (fixed JSX syntax error in fallback breakdown branch lines ~1340-1381 so Metro can bundle; added Base-fee row that was missing its label)

success_rate.frontend: 95% (Phase C wiring 100%, only submit not end-to-end re-captured)

context_for_next_testing_agent: The shipper-create.tsx is huge and contained a JSX syntax error in the fallback breakdown branch that broke the whole web bundle. After fix it works. Phase C marketplace UI verified via real POST /api/shipper/quote/recommend response. Use demo.shipper@nadaruns.com / demo1234. Token key in localStorage: 'nadaruns_auth_token'. Note: typing addresses manually (without map picker) makes both coords fall back to Helsinki center → distance_km=0 in the quote.
rca_of_issue: Root cause of pre-fix blank-screen: In shipper-create.tsx the ternary `{quote.breakdown_lines ? (...) : (...)}` had its else-branch composed of multiple adjacent JSX elements (a <View> followed by another sibling <View>) without an enclosing parent. Babel/Metro raised 'SyntaxError: Adjacent JSX elements must be wrapped in an enclosing tag' which caused 'Web Bundling failed' for expo-router/entry.js. The browser then loaded the Metro error bundle whose root component crashes with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading map)', producing a blank white page on every route. Reproduction: open any route → blank. Mitigation: wrapped the fallback breakdown into a single <View style={styles.breakdown}> container and added the missing Base-fee row. After fix, Metro bundles cleanly and all routes load.
